task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Create New Order Status' form or the order statuses grid showing Status Code 'quality_check' and Status Label 'Quality Inspection'.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Quality Inspection' to the 'Processing' state with correct visibility
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Assign Order Status to State' form or grid showing 'Quality Inspection' assigned to the 'Processing' state, 'Visible On Storefront' checked/Yes, and 'Use As Default' unchecked/No.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Ready for Pickup' order status
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Create New Order Status' form or the order statuses grid showing Status Code 'ready_pickup' and Status Label 'Ready for Pickup'.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Ready for Pickup' to the 'Complete' state with correct visibility
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Assign Order Status to State' form or grid showing 'Ready for Pickup' assigned to the 'Complete' state, 'Visible On Storefront' checked/Yes, and 'Use As Default' unchecked/No.
Look for the 'Create New Order Status' form or the order statuses grid showing Status Code 'supplier_delay' and Status Label 'Awaiting Supplier'.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Awaiting Supplier' to the 'On Hold' state with correct visibility
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Assign Order Status to State' form or grid showing 'Awaiting Supplier' assigned to the 'On Hold' state, 'Visible On Storefront' unchecked/No, and 'Use As Default' unchecked/No.

task_completion.description: Verify all three custom statuses are present in the Order Statuses grid
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main 'Order Statuses' grid in the Magento Admin panel. It should list 'Quality Inspection', 'Ready for Pickup', and 'Awaiting Supplier' with their respective assigned states and visibility settings.

integrity.description: Agent used the Magento Admin GUI to create and assign statuses
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Magento Admin panel (Stores > Order Status), filling out forms, and clicking save buttons, rather than executing SQL queries or API scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with a real web browser
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should display a standard web browser interface (like Chrome or Firefox) rendering the Magento backend, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not fake the success message or grid using browser developer tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element) should not be open and actively modifying the DOM to fake the presence of the new order statuses.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating three custom order statuses in Magento 2. 1) Code: 'quality_check', Label: 'Quality Inspection', State: 'Processing' (processing), Visible on Storefront: Yes (1), Default: No. 2) Code: 'ready_pickup', Label: 'Ready for Pickup', State: 'Complete' (complete), Visible on Storefront: Yes (1), Default: No. 3) Code: 'supplier_delay', Label: 'Awaiting Supplier', State: 'On Hold' (holded), Visible on Storefront: No (0), Default: No. Ensure none are set as default for their states.
